Mia confirmed the arrangement and announced her transition to Product team, with a new CSM to be introduced. Will expressed interest in exploring AI Search product.

recent.summary: Final weekly sync of 2025. Discussed BFCM performance and New Year campaign plans. Ryan Barry's last call before leaving FERMAT; Mia DyBuncio officially took over as CSM.

calls.analysis: FlavCity had consistent weekly calls from Dec 2025 through March 13, 2026. Last recorded call was March 13 (24 days ago) where the brand requested month-to-month transition at $2K/month with contract expiring April 15. Three CSM transitions have occurred: Ryan Barry left Dec 2025, Mia DyBuncio took over and then moved to Product team in March 2026, Kaitlin Frye was briefly introduced as replacement. Pulkit is now the assigned CSM as of late March. 9 calls recorded in Glyphic total. Key brand contacts: Will Matalene (Head of Growth), Christy Hernandez (new D2C lead since March), Marie Leroux (operations). Agency partners: Chief Detective (Ryan Longo, Eddie), Ecko Digital Media (Sammie Walker), Y'all (Travis Halff). TikTok agency contact SarahJay from Verb.tech departed in late February.

calendar.analysis: No FlavCity calendar events found between March 9 and June 7, 2026. Searched 'FlavCity', 'Shop FlavCity', and 'Flavcity Fermat' -- all returned zero results. The weekly sync cadence has lapsed since the CSM transition. A new recurring meeting needs to be established with the FlavCity team.

slack.analysis: Slack channel is now actively re-engaging as of April 6. Pulkit joined the channel March 27 and sent his first outreach on April 6, receiving fast responses from Will (18 min) and Christy (same day). Marie is actively building new funnels and asking technical questions (subscription LP, module ordering). Key engagement pattern: Will is the primary interlocutor on strategic matters (contract, agency coordination, Amazon pricing questions, AI Search), Marie handles funnel building and technical implementation, Christy is new and asking to join calls. The channel was quiet from mid-March through early April during the CSM transition gap. Historical activity shows strong engagement from Will with multiple channel invite requests and strategic questions. Agency partners Chief Detective and Y'all are connected in the channel.

messages.to: Will Matalene <will@shopflavcity.com>, Christy Hernandez <christy@shopflavcity.com>, Marie Leroux <marie@shopflavcity.com>
messages.subject: FlavCity x FERMAT -- Loving the TikTok Traffic, Let's Chat Strategy
messages.summary: Pulkit's first email outreach to FlavCity. Highlighted massive TikTok session volume (millions) across Butter Coffee, Cinnamon Roll, Chocolate, Mint Chocolate funnels. Noted FlavCity is driving more traffic than every other brand on FERMAT combined. Flagged low CVR on TikTok funnels vs higher-converting Spring 30 funnel. Proposed call to discuss optimization opportunities. Shared calendar scheduling link.

email.analysis: Pulkit sent his first email outreach to FlavCity on April 6 (same day as Slack message). Email went to Will, Christy, and Marie with Mukul CC'd. The email mirrors the Slack message content about TikTok traffic volume and CVR optimization opportunity. No email reply received yet as of April 6. No other email threads found in the last 60 days, indicating communication has historically been Slack and call-driven. The email was a coordinated outreach alongside the Slack message as Pulkit's opening engagement as the new CSM.

mentions.context: FlavCity discussed during CSM Standup. Main issues: massive TikTok session volume with near-zero CVR, suspected organic/viral traffic not paid. Billing discrepancy flagged -- brand on $2,000 plan but internal records show $1,500. Team decided to first understand the traffic source, then address the cost conversation. Pulkit to use Will's request to connect as an opening.
FlavCity onboarding discussed. Calendar invite and platform demo to be scheduled. Monthly check-ins preferred over bi-weekly. Payment processing still pending.
Pulkit tasked to get update from Mia on FlavCity and send message + email to the brand.

granola.analysis: No dedicated FlavCity meetings recorded in Granola (meetings with FlavCity contacts were pre-Pulkit era and recorded in Glyphic). FlavCity was discussed in 3 internal meetings: Apr 6 CSM Standup (traffic anomaly, billing discrepancy, outreach plan), Mar 30 Weekly Check-in (Pulkit assigned to reach out), Mar 16 CSM Standup (onboarding planning, monthly cadence preference). The team is treating FlavCity as a re-engagement project with a careful approach to the billing/cost conversation.

notes.contract: Current annual contract expires April 15, 2026. Brand requested month-to-month transition at $2,000/month via Stripe (confirmed in March 13 call). Internal billing discrepancy: plan shows $2,000 but internal records show $1,500.

notes.csm_history: Ryan Barry (CSM until Dec 2025) -> Mia DyBuncio (CSM Dec 2025 - Mar 2026, moved to Product) -> Kaitlin Frye (brief handoff in March) -> Pulkit Srivastava (current CSM, joined channel Mar 27, first outreach Apr 6)
notes.strategic_context: FlavCity is a health-focused food/beverage brand with strong TikTok presence. Massive organic TikTok traffic driving millions of sessions through flavor-specific funnels (Butter Coffee, Cinnamon Roll, Chocolate, Mint Chocolate, Vanilla). Traffic is intentional per Will -- redirected from D2C to avoid unqualified traffic. Large TikTok Shop program planned for end of May. Ad strategy shifted from trial kits to full-price bags with GLP1/breakfast replacement angles. AI Search onboarding completed in January. Subscription offer testing was planned but status unclear post-CSM transitions.
